message.content: Update the existing statusline script at /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh to remove the @hostname from the user@host segment. It should show only the username (e.g., "garrosh" instead of "garrosh@hostname"). Read the script first to find the exact line to change.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Claude Code status line — mirrors your p10k prompt
3	# Elements: user@host  cwd  git  time | model  usage_bar  ctx_bar
4	
5	input=$(cat)
6	
7	cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
8	model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
9	used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
10	five_hour_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
11	
12	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	# make_bar PERCENT [WIDTH]
14	#   Renders a compact block-character progress bar with Naysayer color coding.
15	#   Color thresholds: <50% linefg #126367 | 50-79% warn #ffaa00 | 80%+ red
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	make_bar() {
18	  local pct="$1"
19	  local width="${2:-10}"
20	  local pct_int
21	  pct_int=$(printf '%.0f' "$pct")
22	
23	  # Pick color based on threshold
24	  local r g b
25	  if [ "$pct_int" -ge 80 ]; then
26	    r=255; g=0;   b=0
27	  elif [ "$pct_int" -ge 50 ]; then
28	    r=255; g=170; b=0
29	  else
30	    r=18;  g=99;  b=103
31	  fi
32	
33	  # Block characters in eighths: ▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█ (plus space for empty)
34	  local eighths_chars=' ▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█'
35	
36	  # Total eighths filled = pct * width / 100 * 8
37	  local total_eighths=$(( pct_int * width * 8 / 100 ))
38	  local full_blocks=$(( total_eighths / 8 ))
39	  local remainder=$(( total_eighths % 8 ))
40	
41	  local bar=""
42	  local i=0
43	  while [ $i -lt $full_blocks ]; do
44	    bar="${bar}█"
45	    i=$(( i + 1 ))
46	  done
47	
48	  # Partial block (skip if remainder is 0 or bar is already full)
49	  if [ $remainder -gt 0 ] && [ $full_blocks -lt $width ]; then
50	    # Extract the nth character from eighths_chars (0-indexed, 0=space … 8=█)
51	    partial=$(printf '%s' "$eighths_chars" | cut -c$(( remainder + 1 )))
52	    bar="${bar}${partial}"
53	    full_blocks=$(( full_blocks + 1 ))
54	  fi
55	
56	  # Pad with spaces to reach full width
57	  while [ $full_blocks -lt $width ]; do
58	    bar="${bar} "
59	    full_blocks=$(( full_blocks + 1 ))
60	  done
61	
62	  # Wrap bar in color, append percentage label
63	  printf '\033[38;2;%d;%d;%dm[%s] %d%%\033[0m' "$r" "$g" "$b" "$bar" "$pct_int"
64	}
65	
66	# user@host (linefg #126367)
67	user_host=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)")
68	
69	# cwd (blue #66D9EF) — abbreviate $HOME to ~
70	display_cwd="${cwd/#$HOME/\~}"
71	dir_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;102;217;239m%s\033[0m' "$display_cwd")
72	
73	# git info (linefg / warn on dirty)
74	git_part=""
75	if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
76	  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
77	  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
78	    # check dirty
79	    if ! git -C "$cwd" diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
80	      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[38;2;255;170;0m*\033[0m' "$branch")
81	    else
82	      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$branch")
83	    fi
84	  fi
85	fi
86	
87	# time (fg #d0b892)
88	time_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;208;184;146m%s\033[0m' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)")
89	
90	# model (subtle)
91	model_part=""
92	[ -n "$model" ] && model_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$model")
93	
94	# usage bar — 5-hour API rate limit (only shown when data is available)
95	usage_bar_part=""
96	if [ -n "$five_hour_pct" ]; then
97	  usage_bar_part=" $(make_bar "$five_hour_pct" 10)"
98	  # Prepend a dim label
99	  usage_bar_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103mapi:\033[0m%s' "$usage_bar_part")
100	fi
101	
102	# context bar — context window usage (only shown when data is available)
103	ctx_bar_part=""
104	if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
105	  ctx_bar_part=" $(make_bar "$used_pct" 10)"
106	  ctx_bar_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103mctx:\033[0m%s' "$ctx_bar_part")
107	fi
108	
109	# Compose line
110	line="${user_host}  ${dir_part}${git_part}  ${time_part}"
111	if [ -n "$model_part" ]; then
112	  line="${line}  ${model_part}"
113	  [ -n "$usage_bar_part" ] && line="${line}  ${usage_bar_part}"
114	  [ -n "$ctx_bar_part" ]   && line="${line}  ${ctx_bar_part}"
115	fi
116	
117	printf '%s\n' "$line"
118

content.text: Line 67 is the one to change. The `%s@%s` format and `$(hostname -s)` argument need to be removed so only the username is shown.

content.content: The file /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Done. Line 67 in `/home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh` has been updated:

- Before: `printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)"`
- After: `printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)"`

The status line will now show just the username (e.g., `garrosh`) instead of `garrosh@hostname`. The color remains unchanged.

Note: for any further status line changes, the `statusline-setup` agent should be used. You can also just ask Claude directly to make additional tweaks and it will handle them for you.
